_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to blend sounds together to say a word, and break a word apart into its separate sounds.

_source.w: We are learning to blend sounds together to say a word, and break a word apart into its separate sounds.
y: Blending sounds is how we read — we put the sounds together to hear the word.
Segmenting sounds is how we spell — we hear the word and write each sound.
Mastering this skill is one of the biggest steps towards becoming a reader and writer.
eg: We hear 'c-a-n' and blend the sounds together to say 'can'.
We hear the word 'ship' and break it apart into 'sh-i-p'.
We use sound boxes to push a counter for each sound we hear in a word.
We practise blending and segmenting with a partner — one person says the sounds, the other says the word.
We use magnetic letters to build a word sound by sound.
We play 'robot talk' where we say words in a segmented, robotic way and then blend them.
